identity.title: LNA Gain Compression Macro
identity.supported_scope: pure voltage-domain event-driven transient behavioral Verilog-A

parameters.name: vth
parameters.override_behavior: sets clk and rst logic threshold

parameters.name: gain
parameters.override_behavior: sets small-signal gain about 0.45 V common mode

properties.observable_contract: Initialization sets out to 0.45 V and clears metric; an active-high reset sampled on a rising clk crossing restores the same state.
For linear values from 0.14 V through 0.76 V, out equals 0.45 V plus gain times the sampled vin deviation and metric is 0.1 V.
Above linear 0.76 V, excess signal is compressed by factor 0.28 and metric is 0.8 V.
Below linear 0.14 V, excess signal is compressed by factor 0.28 and metric is 0.8 V.
The final held output remains within 0.04 V through 0.86 V.
Out and metric update on rising clock crossings and hold between samples.

modeling_constraints: Use deterministic rising-edge sampled piecewise gain compression.
Do not use current contributions, transistor-level devices, AC/noise analysis, KCL/KVL assumptions, or validation side channels.
